concerns.text: Task T017 implements imputation using 'Structure-based KNN (Morgan fingerprints)' and 'SMILES derived from composition'. This violates FR-003 which explicitly mandates 'Euclidean distance in stoichiometry space (normalized element counts)'. The task silently relaxes the mathematical definition of the distance metric required by the spec, changing the algorithm from stoichiometry-based to structure-based without explicit spec authorization.

Task T014 implements alignment using only 'composition' and 'surface_facet' and explicitly omits 'synthesis_condition'. FR-002 mandates alignment on all three columns ('composition', 'surface_facet', 'synthesis_condition') and defines exclusion logic for missing 'synthesis_condition'. The task weakens the alignment constraint by removing a required column, relying on a plan-side 'scope adjustment' rather than a spec amendment.

Tasks T028a and T028b implement a Shapiro-Wilk test on the distribution of 'paired differences' (XGBoost error - Baseline error). FR-005 explicitly requires the Shapiro-Wilk test to be performed on the 'distribution of absolute errors' to determine normality before choosing the statistical test. The tasks implement a different statistical check (normality of differences vs normality of errors), violating the spec's methodological requirement.

Task T012 documents the exclusion of Materials Project and 2025 CO2 study datasets. While the plan justifies this, FR-001 explicitly mandates downloading these datasets. The task implements a scope reduction (dropping FR-001 requirements) without a corresponding amendment to the spec. This is a silent weakening of the functional requirement to fetch specific external sources.
